subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **200** word cards (double-sided)
- **1** card stand
- **11** green agent cards
- **1** double agent card
- **3** assassin cards
- **7** innocent bystander cards
- **1** timer token
- **11** timer tokens (numbered)
- **Key cards** (double-sided with different grids per side)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Lay out 25 word cards** in a 5×5 grid.
2. **Place a key card** in the stand between players — each player sees their own side.
3. **Place timer tokens** — Start with 9 tokens (adjustable for difficulty).
4. **Each player** sees different information on the key card.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Sit across from each other** with the key card stand between you.
- **Each player's key card side** shows 9 green agents, 3 assassins, and 13 bystanders — but the grids differ per side.
- **3 agents overlap** — Both sides share some green positions.
- **15 total agents** to find (9 + 9 − 3 shared = 15).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Codenames: Duet is a cooperative 2-player word association game. Take turns giving one-word clues to help your partner identify secret agents among the 25 words. Find all 15 agents before running out of turns or hitting an assassin.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Players alternate as clue-giver and guesser:

1. **Give a clue** — Say one word and one number. The word relates to agent words on your side of the key. The number tells how many words relate.
2. **Partner guesses** — Touch word cards one at a time:
   - **Green (agent)** — Correct! Cover with green card. May continue guessing.
   - **Beige (bystander)** — Wrong. Turn ends. Remove 1 timer token.
   - **Black (assassin)** — Instant loss!
3. **Partner may stop guessing** at any time (costs 1 timer token).
4. **Switch roles** — The other player now gives a clue.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Clue Rules
- **One word only** — Your clue must be a single word.
- **Number** — Indicates how many words on the grid relate to your clue.
- **No pointing, gestures, or extra hints**.
- **Can't use** any word visible on the grid as a clue.

#### Timer Tokens
- Start with 9 (standard difficulty).
- Remove 1 when your partner guesses a bystander or voluntarily stops.
- When all tokens are gone, you get one final turn each.

#### Sudden Death
- After timer tokens run out, each player gets 1 final turn.
- You must correctly guess at least 1 agent or you lose.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Overlapping agents** — 3 positions are agents on both sides. When found, they count for both players.
- **Assassins differ per side** — A word that's an assassin for one player might be a bystander for the other.
- **Campaign mode** — Play through a series of missions with increasing difficulty (fewer timer tokens, more assassins).
- **Unlimited guesses** — You may guess up to the clue number + 1 (to catch up on previous clues).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win
- Find all 15 agents before running out of timer tokens and final turns.

#### Lose
- **Assassin** — Either player guesses an assassin word.
- **Time out** — Timer tokens depleted and final turns used without finding all agents.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Avoid assassins** — Check your side carefully. Clues that could lead to assassin words are dangerous.
- **Multi-word clues** — Try to link 2-3 words per clue for efficiency.
- **Trust your partner** — If they give a clue you're unsure about, think harder before guessing randomly.
- **Save tokens** — Don't guess wildly. Stopping voluntarily is better than hitting a bystander.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Prioritize assassin avoidance** — Your clue must not lead your partner toward words that are assassins on their side.
- **Efficient clues** — You need to average 2+ words per clue to win with standard tokens.
- **Read the board** — Consider what your partner's clues tell you about their side of the key.
- **Campaign scaling** — In harder missions, be more conservative and precise with clues.
